title: 3 bedroom houses for rent in Ohio City
metaTitle: 3 Bedroom Houses for Rent in Ohio City, Cleveland
metaDescription: Searching for 3 bedroom houses for rent in Ohio City, Cleveland? See typical rents, housing stock, transit, and Section 8-friendly homes. Book a showing.
primaryKeyword: 3 bedroom houses for rent Ohio City cleveland
answerBox: Three-bedroom houses in Ohio City, Cleveland's near-west side neighborhood in ZIP 44113, are typically older single-family or duplex-style homes near the West Side Market and the RTA Red Line's Ohio City station. Citywide, Zumper's July 2026 report puts median 3-bedroom rent at $1,350; our own recent 3-bedroom Cleveland listings have ranged roughly $1,100–$1,500.

sections.h2: 3-bedroom houses for rent in Ohio City, Cleveland
body: Ohio City is a near-west side Cleveland neighborhood in **ZIP code 44113**, best known as home to the historic West Side Market and as the site of the RTA Red Line's Ohio City station. For renters who need three bedrooms rather than a smaller apartment, Ohio City's housing stock includes a mix of 19th- and early-20th-century single-family homes and two-family duplexes, some with a larger unit that includes the full third bedroom on an upper floor.

We manage voucher-friendly rental homes in and around Ohio City as part of our broader Cleveland portfolio. [Book a free showing](/p/book-showing) to see what's currently available, or [tell our team what you need](/p/book-showing) if you don't see a 3-bedroom listed yet.

sections.h2: What does a 3-bedroom home look like in Ohio City?
body: Much of Ohio City's residential stock predates 1930, with narrow-lot frame and brick homes, front porches, and small yards typical of Cleveland's near-west side neighborhoods. Three-bedroom configurations here are often a full single-family house or the larger of two units in a converted duplex, sometimes with an English basement or finished attic adding extra square footage. Because the neighborhood grew up around streetcar and market commerce, most homes sit within walking distance of Lorain Avenue or W. 25th Street retail corridors rather than in a purely residential subdivision.

Renters comparing a 3-bedroom Ohio City home to a similarly sized suburban rental should factor in the neighborhood's age of construction — expect features like a full basement, a detached garage or on-street parking, and gas or hot-water heating typical of pre-war Cleveland housing, rather than the central air and attached garages more common in newer suburban builds.

sections.h2: How much does a 3-bedroom house cost in Ohio City?
body: Ohio City sits within the City of Cleveland, so citywide market data is the most reliable published benchmark for this ZIP. Zumper's report dated July 4, 2026 lists the City of Cleveland's median 3-bedroom asking rent at $1,350 a month, up 4.2% year-over-year overall. RentCafe's report dated July 2, 2026, which tracks larger professionally managed apartment communities, lists an average 3-bedroom rent of $2,716 for about 1,483 square feet — notably higher because it reflects newer, amenity-heavy buildings rather than older neighborhood housing stock. Our own recently available 3-bedroom homes across Cleveland have typically rented from about $1,100 to $1,500 a month; check current availability for Ohio City specifically since inventory changes.

rows: Citywide asking-rent median, all listing types

body: Ohio City is served directly by the RTA Red Line's Ohio City station, the only heavy-rail line in Greater Cleveland and the one that runs from Cleveland Hopkins Airport through Downtown to East Cleveland. The neighborhood also sits near the Hope Memorial Bridge into Downtown and has direct access to I-90. The West Side Market, on W. 25th Street at Lorain Avenue, anchors the neighborhood's day-to-day retail and grocery needs.

Several GCRTA bus routes also run through Ohio City, connecting it to the near-west side neighborhoods of Detroit-Shoreway and Tremont as well as Downtown's Tower City hub, where the Red, Blue, Green, and Waterfront rail lines converge. For a household renting a 3-bedroom home, that direct rail access can matter more than it would for a smaller unit, since larger households often need to coordinate multiple commutes to work, school, or medical appointments across the city.

sections.h2: Section 8 vouchers for 3-bedroom homes in Ohio City
body: Every home Rent Finder Cleveland manages accepts Section 8 Housing Choice Vouchers and is HUD-inspection-ready, including our 3-bedroom listings. Ohio has no statewide law requiring landlords to accept vouchers, and the City of Cleveland has not enacted a local source-of-income protection, so not every landlord in Ohio City will accept a voucher — ours do. See our full [Section 8 housing guide](/section-8-housing-cleveland-oh/) for how CMHA vouchers and payment standards work.

faq.q: Are there 3-bedroom houses for rent in Ohio City, Cleveland?
faq.a: Yes. Ohio City's housing stock includes single-family homes and duplexes with 3-bedroom configurations, often on upper floors of converted two-family buildings. Availability changes regularly, so book a showing or tell our team what you need and we'll follow up with current options.

faq.q: How much does a 3-bedroom house rent for in Ohio City?
faq.a: Zumper's July 2026 report puts the City of Cleveland's median 3-bedroom rent at $1,350/month. Our own recently available 3-bedroom homes across Cleveland have typically ranged from about $1,100 to $1,500, depending on condition and location within the neighborhood.

faq.q: What ZIP code is Ohio City in?
faq.a: Ohio City is in ZIP code 44113, on Cleveland's near-west side, home to the West Side Market and the RTA Red Line's Ohio City station.

faq.q: Do Ohio City rentals accept Section 8 vouchers?
faq.a: Every home Rent Finder Cleveland manages, including our 3-bedroom listings, accepts Housing Choice Vouchers and is HUD-inspection-ready. Ohio has no statewide law requiring all landlords to accept vouchers, so confirm voucher acceptance with any other landlord directly.
